title: guarea macrophylla subsp. spiciflora
intro: Guarea macrophylla subsp. spiciflora, also known as West African mango or bush mango, is a plant species native to tropical Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Guarea macrophylla subsp. spiciflora include treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as providing antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. In herbal preparations, the plant's bark, leaves, and seeds are used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions.
